description: Quantization efficiency benchmark. Measures throughput and accuracy across BF16, FP16, FP8, W8A8, W8A16, and W4A16 precision formats using fixed pre-quantized checkpoints. FP16 is included as a hardware-compatibility baseline for pre-Ampere GPUs.
_precision_note: precision_required/allowed_precisions are used for run_id stability and as a safety net if _resolve_precision is called unexpectedly. The actual per-format precision is controlled by precision_model_map — each format runs as an explicit subprocess with --precision set.

FP16._note: Same checkpoint as BF16 — dtype_override forces float16 compute. Required for pre-Ampere GPUs (V100, T4) that lack native BF16 support.

FP8._note: Static per-tensor FP8 (weights + activations). Requires Ampere+ (A100, A800, H20). Skipped automatically on FP16-only hardware.

W8A8._note: INT8 weights + INT8 activations via compressed-tensors. Exercises native int8 tensor cores.

W8A16._note: INT8 weights, FP16 activations. Weight-only quantization — reduces memory bandwidth, not compute dtype.

W4A16._note: INT4 weights, FP16 activations via GPTQ Marlin kernels. Weight-only quantization — larger memory saving than W8A16.

_precision_levels_note: FP16 runs on all hardware including pre-Ampere. FP8 requires Ampere+ and is skipped automatically on FP16-only runners via SUPPORTED_QUANTIZATION_BACKENDS.
_request_count_note: offline uses request_count (100) per format - 5 formats × 4 concurrency levels, so keeping this low is important for total runtime; online (extra) uses online_request_count (300, robust p99)
